message:
fix(fnp-python): take out-of-range index raises IndexError (numpy parity)

Our in-Rust take path returned UFuncError::Msg("take: index X out of
bounds ...") which map_ufunc_error converted to PyValueError. numpy.take
raises IndexError (not ValueError) — user code catching IndexError
specifically would fail to catch ours.

Fix: when a.take() returns Err, fall back to numpy.take so the exception
type (IndexError), message, and traceback match numpy exactly. The
happy path keeps the in-Rust optimization.

Added tests::take_index_out_of_bounds_matches_numpy_indexerror as the
regression gate for this error-type parity contract.
